+# local-alexa-ticktick-bridge: Alexa Shopping List to TickTick Bridge
+
+## Original Request
+
+Complete the AI-DLC planning phase for workspace `/[REDACTED]-ticktick-bridge/worktrees/local-alexa-ticktick-bridge` using `/[REDACTED].md` as the read-only source specification. The user wants implementation under root `worktrees/` and wants the workflow to stop before git commit, push, root-export, or cleanup.
+
+## Scope Summary
+
+Implement a private Python 3.12 package named `alexa_ticktick_bridge` and a Typer CLI named `atb` in repo `alexa_ticktick_bridge/`.
+
+The bridge polls Alexa Shopping List items, creates TickTick tasks in a configured project, optionally notifies Slack, marks the Alexa item `COMPLETE` only after successful TickTick creation, and records state in SQLite so reruns are idempotent.
+
+The implementation must not depend on or copy `pyalexatodo`. Alexa list operations are self-implemented. Amazon login may use `aioamazondevices` only behind `AmazonAuthProvider` and `AuthenticatedAmazonSession` adapter boundaries.
+
+## Non-Goals
+
+- No publishing to PyPI.
+- No official Alexa List Management REST API dependency.
+- No Alexa item deletion in the sync path.
+- No Amazon password persistence by default.
+- No live Amazon test in CI.
+- No commit, push, root-export, or cleanup without explicit user approval.
+- No plan writer creation of work items.
+
+## Public Interfaces And Behavior
+
+### Package And CLI
+
+- Python package: `alexa_ticktick_bridge`
+- Console script: `atb`
+- Python: `>=3.12`
+- Runtime dependencies expected by the source spec: `aiohttp`, `aioamazondevices`, `pydantic`, `typer`, `rich`, `keyring`, `structlog`
+- Dev dependencies expected by the source spec: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `aioresponses`, `ruff`, `mypy`, `pip-audit`
+
+Expected repository layout:
+
+```text
+alexa_ticktick_bridge/
+  pyproject.toml
+  README.md
+  SECURITY.md
+  .env.example
+  src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/
+  tests/
+```
+
+The source spec names package directories directly at repo root. The repo worker may choose `src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/` if consistent with `pyproject.toml`; tests and CLI imports must match.
+
+### CLI Commands
+
+The implementation should expose these Typer commands unless a work item explicitly stages them:
+
+```text
+atb init
+atb config show
+atb secret set ticktick.client_secret
+atb secret set slack.webhook_url
+atb secret delete <key>
+atb ticktick login
+atb ticktick projects
+atb ticktick test-create "test item"
+atb amazon login
+atb amazon lists
+atb amazon items
+atb amazon complete --list-id <id> --item-id <id> --version <version>
+atb sync once
+atb sync once --dry-run
+atb daemon
+atb status
+atb failed
+atb retry --alexa-item-id <id>
+```
+
+`--show-names` or equivalent explicit opt-in is required before CLI output displays full shopping item names for diagnostic commands. Logs redact names by default.
+
+### Config
+
+Default config path:
+
+```text
+~/.config/alexa-ticktick-bridge/config.toml
+```
+
+Default state paths:
+
+```text
+~/.local/share/alexa-ticktick-bridge/state.db
+~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock
+```
+
+Config model must include:
+
+- `poll.interval_seconds`, default `180`, allowed `60..180`
+- `poll.jitter_seconds`, default `15`
+- `poll.max_backoff_seconds`, default `1800`
+- `amazon.domain`, default `co.jp`
+- `amazon.list_type`, default `SHOP`
+- `amazon.store_password`, default `false`
+- `ticktick.client_id`
+- `ticktick.redirect_uri`, default `http://127.0.0.1:8765/callback`
+- `ticktick.scope`, default `tasks:read tasks:write`
+- `ticktick.project_id`
+- `slack.enabled`, default `false`
+- `slack.redact_item_name`, default `false`
+- `storage.sqlite_path`
+- `storage.store_plain_item_names`, default `true`
+- `logging.level`, default `INFO`
+- `logging.redact_item_names`, default `true`
+
+### SecretStore
+
+Define a `SecretStore` interface with `get`, `set`, `delete`, `get_json`, and `set_json`. The default implementation uses OS keyring service `alexa-ticktick-bridge`.
+
+Secret keys:
+
+```text
+amazon.email
+amazon.login_data
+amazon.password
+ticktick.client_secret
+ticktick.token_response
+slack.webhook_url
+```
+
+If keyring is unavailable, fail closed by default. Plain secret files are allowed only behind an explicit unsafe flag or command option. Amazon password is stored only when `amazon.store_password=[REDACTED] or an explicit CLI flag requests it.
+
+### TickTick OAuth And Client
+
+Implement authorization-code OAuth for TickTick:
+
+- authorize URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/authorize`
+- token URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/token`
+- local callback binds only to `127.0.0.1`
+- callback must validate `state`
+- token exchange uses form data and Basic Auth with client id and client secret
+- token response is stored in SecretStore as `ticktick.token_response`
+
+Implement a TickTick client for:
+
+- `GET /open/v1/project`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}/data` for debug or duplicate investigation
+- `POST /open/v1/task`
+
+MVP does not need TickTick completion, deletion, or update in the sync path.
+
+### Amazon Auth Adapter
+
+Define adapter interfaces:
+
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.domain`
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.request_json(method, url, payload)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.login_interactive(email, password, otp)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.restore()`
+
+MVP implementation may use `aioamazondevices` for login and stored login restore only. Any private-member use required by `aioamazondevices` must remain isolated inside the adapter implementation. The Alexa list client must not import or depend on `aioamazondevices` directly.
+
+Authentication behavior:
+
+- `atb amazon login` prompts for email, password, and current OTP
+- successful login stores `amazon.email` and `amazon.login_data`
+- default behavior does not store `amazon.password`
+- restore uses stored login data
+- restore failure raises `ReauthRequired` and daemon exits with a clear reauth message
+
+### Alexa Lists Client
+
+Implement `AlexaListsClient` against the non-official Alexa mobile-app-style list endpoints described in the source spec:
+
+- list fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/fetch`
+- items fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/fetch?limit=100`
+- complete item: `PUT /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/{itemId}?version={version}`
+
+Models should represent:
+
+- `AlexaListType`: `SHOP`, `TODO`, `CUSTOM`
+- `AlexaItemStatus`: `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETE`
+- `AlexaList`: `listId`, `listType`, `listName`
+- `AlexaItem`: `itemId`, `itemStatus`, `itemName`, `version`
+
+`get_shop_list()` returns the `SHOP` list or raises a typed bridge error. `complete_item()` only updates status to `COMPLETE`; sync must not delete Alexa items.
+
+### Slack Client
+
+Implement Slack Incoming Webhook posting with webhook URL loaded from SecretStore. The webhook URL is never logged or stored in config.
+
+Slack notification failure must not cause duplicate TickTick tasks and must not block Alexa completion after TickTick creation. Rows with missing `slack_notified_at` may be retried later.
+
+### SQLite Store
+
+Create and migrate a SQLite database containing `sync_items` with at least:
+
+- `alexa_item_id` primary key
+- `alexa_list_id`
+- `alexa_item_name`
+- `alexa_item_name_hash`
+- `alexa_version`
+- `ticktick_project_id`
+- `ticktick_task_id`
+- `state`
+- `ticktick_created_at`
+- `slack_notified_at`
+- `alexa_completed_at`
+- `error_count`
+- `last_error`
+- `first_seen_at`
+- `updated_at`
+
+Required states:
+
+```text
+DISCOVERED
+TICKTICK_CREATED
+SLACK_NOTIFIED
+ALEXA_COMPLETED
+FAILED_RETRYABLE
+FAILED_FATAL
+```
+
+The store must support idempotent upsert, mark TickTick created, mark Slack notified, mark Alexa completed, record retryable/fatal errors, and query failed records for status/retry commands.
+
+### SyncService
+
+Required sync order:
+
+1. restore Amazon session
+2. load TickTick token
+3. get Alexa `SHOP` list
+4. get Alexa list items
+5. filter `ACTIVE` items
+6. upsert discovered SQLite record
+7. create TickTick task if `ticktick_task_id` is missing
+8. notify Slack if enabled and not already notified
+9. mark Alexa item `COMPLETE`
+10. mark DB record `ALEXA_COMPLETED`
+
+Idempotency rules:
+
+- `ALEXA_COMPLETED` rows are skipped.
+- Existing rows with `ticktick_task_id` must not create another TickTick task.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Slack fails, rerun must retry Slack without duplicating TickTick.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Alexa complete fails, rerun must complete Alexa without duplicating TickTick.
+- `--dry-run` must not create TickTick tasks, post Slack messages, complete Alexa items, or mutate external systems.
+
+TickTick task content should include sync metadata:
+
+```text
+Synced from Alexa Shopping List
+
+alexa_item_id: <id>
+alexa_list_id: <id>
+alexa_version_at_sync: <version>
+synced_by: alexa_ticktick_bridge
+```
+
+### Scheduler And Locking
+
+`atb daemon` uses `SyncService.run_once()` in a loop:
+
+- default interval is 180 seconds
+- supported poll interval range is 60 to 180 seconds
+- jitter is applied
+- 429, 5xx, and network errors use exponential backoff capped by config
+- `ReauthRequired` stops the daemon and asks for login
+- concurrent `atb daemon` or `atb sync once` runs are blocked by a file lock at `~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock`
+
+On Unix, `fcntl.flock` is sufficient. Windows lock support is not required for MVP.
+
+### Logging And Redaction
+
+Use structured logs and central redaction helpers. Logs must not contain:
+
+- full Amazon email
+- Amazon password
+- OTP
+- Amazon cookie or login data
+- TickTick token
+- Slack webhook URL
+- full item names by default
+
+Provide tests for email masking, item-name masking, and secret redaction.
+
+### Errors
+
+Define typed errors at least:
+
+- `BridgeError`
+- `ReauthRequired`
+- `AuthFailed`
+- `RateLimited`
+- `RetryableAPIError`
+- `FatalConfigError`
+- `VersionConflict`
+
+Expected handling:
+
+- Amazon stored login failure: `ReauthRequired`
+- Amazon/TickTick 429: backoff
+- network/5xx: retryable
+- TickTick 401: `ReauthRequired`
+- Slack failure: warning and retry later
+- Alexa complete failure: `FAILED_RETRYABLE`
+- permanent config/auth issues: `FAILED_FATAL` or explicit CLI error
+
+## Recommended Work-Item Slices For dlc_scope_manager
+
+The scope manager should create work items, not the plan writer. A feasible one-pass repo worker assignment may combine these if the implementation budget allows; otherwise split in this order:
+
+1. Scaffold package and tooling: `pyproject.toml`, source layout, CLI shell, config, errors, logging, SecretStore, README/SECURITY, baseline tests.
+2. TickTick OAuth/client: OAuth URL, callback server, token exchange, project listing, task creation, CLI commands, tests.
+3. Amazon auth adapter and Alexa list client: adapter boundaries, login/restore, list/item fetch, complete item, CLI commands, tests with fixtures.
+4. SQLite store and SyncService: migrations, idempotent transitions, dry-run, retry state, tests.
+5. Slack, scheduler, lock, hardening: webhook client, daemon, backoff/jitter, file lock, redaction coverage, operational docs.
+
+If scope manager chooses one repo-worker pass, the work item must still require focused checkpoints for each slice and should gate live-network behavior behind explicit user approval.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `pyalexatodo` is not a dependency and no `pyalexatodo` code is copied.
+- `aioamazondevices` imports are isolated to Amazon auth adapter implementation.
+- `AlexaListsClient` is self-implemented against the endpoints in this plan.
+- `atb` CLI exposes init, config, secret, TickTick, Amazon, sync, daemon, status, failed, and retry commands at practical MVP depth.
+- Config and state paths match this plan.
+- SecretStore defaults to keyring and fails closed if unavailable.
+- Amazon password is not persisted by default.
+- TickTick task is created before Alexa completion.
+- Alexa items are completed, not deleted, after successful TickTick creation.
+- Sync is idempotent across reruns and partial failures.
+- Slack failure does not duplicate TickTick tasks.
+- `--dry-run` performs no external mutation.
+- Poll interval is never below 60 seconds.
+- Logs redact secrets and item names by default.
+- Tests cover OAuth URL/state/token exchange behavior, TickTick client parsing and errors, Alexa client parsing and complete payload, SQLite transitions, SyncService idempotency, dry-run, Slack failure handling, file lock behavior where practical, and redaction.
+
+## Test Plan
+
+Repo worker should add automated tests and verifier should run the relevant commands for the final implementation state:
+
+```bash
+ruff check .
+mypy .
+pytest
+pip-audit
+```
+
+Use mocked HTTP for TickTick, Alexa, and Slack unit tests. Live TickTick tests require explicit environment variables and user approval. Amazon live tests are manual only and must not run in CI.
+
+Suggested fixture coverage:
+
+- Alexa list response with `SHOP` and `TODO`
+- Alexa items response with `ACTIVE` and `COMPLETE`
+- TickTick projects response
+- TickTick task create response
+- Slack webhook `ok` response and failure response
+
+## Approval Gates
+
+The workflow may proceed autonomously through planning, scoping, implementation, local verification, evaluation, and handoff within assignment scopes.
+
+Explicit user approval is required before:
+
+- installing/downloading dependencies if not already available
+- contacting live TickTick, Amazon, or Slack services
+- storing real credentials in keyring during any manual run
+- git commit
+- git push
+- PR creation
+- root-export
+- overlay cleanup or destructive cleanup
+
+The requested stop boundary is before commit, push, root-export, and cleanup.
+
+## Assumptions And Risks
+
+- The source spec intentionally targets non-official Alexa list endpoints, so Amazon behavior may change without notice.
+- `aioamazondevices` may expose login internals that are not stable; adapter isolation is mandatory.
+- TickTick API details should be verified against current docs before live use because external APIs can drift.
+- Current repo has only README/LICENSE/gitignore at planning time, so implementation is effectively greenfield.
+- Work-items are intentionally empty until `dlc_scope_manager` creates them.

# local-alexa-ticktick-bridge: Alexa Shopping List to TickTick Bridge

## Original Request

Complete the AI-DLC planning phase for workspace `/[REDACTED]-ticktick-bridge/worktrees/local-alexa-ticktick-bridge` using `/[REDACTED].md` as the read-only source specification. Implementation should proceed under root `worktrees/` and stop before git commit, push, root-export, or cleanup.

## Scope Summary

Implement a private Python 3.12 package named `alexa_ticktick_bridge` and a Typer CLI named `atb` in repo `alexa_ticktick_bridge/`.

The bridge polls Alexa Shopping List items, creates TickTick tasks in a configured project, optionally notifies Slack, marks the Alexa item `COMPLETE` only after successful TickTick creation, and records state in SQLite so reruns are idempotent.

The implementation must not depend on or copy `pyalexatodo`. Alexa list operations are self-implemented. Amazon login may use `aioamazondevices` only behind `AmazonAuthProvider` and `AuthenticatedAmazonSession` adapter boundaries.

## Non-Goals

- No publishing to PyPI.
- No official Alexa List Management REST API dependency.
- No Alexa item deletion in the sync path.
- No Amazon password persistence by default.
- No live Amazon test in CI.
- No commit, push, root-export, or cleanup without explicit user approval.
- No plan writer creation of work items.

## Public Interfaces And Behavior

### Package And CLI

- Python package: `alexa_ticktick_bridge`
- Console script: `atb`
- Python: `>=3.12`
- Runtime dependencies from the source spec: `aiohttp`, `aioamazondevices`, `pydantic`, `typer`, `rich`, `keyring`, `structlog`
- Dev dependencies from the source spec: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `aioresponses`, `ruff`, `mypy`, `pip-audit`

Expected repository layout:

```text
alexa_ticktick_bridge/
  pyproject.toml
  README.md
  SECURITY.md
  .env.example
  src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/
  tests/
```

The source spec names package directories directly at repo root. The repo worker may choose `src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/` if `pyproject.toml`, tests, and CLI imports are consistent.

### CLI Commands

The implementation should expose these Typer commands unless scope manager intentionally stages them:

```text
atb init
atb config show
atb secret set ticktick.client_secret
atb secret set slack.webhook_url
atb secret delete <key>
atb ticktick login
atb ticktick projects
atb ticktick test-create "test item"
atb amazon login
atb amazon lists
atb amazon items
atb amazon complete --list-id <id> --item-id <id> --version <version>
atb sync once
atb sync once --dry-run
atb daemon
atb status
atb failed
atb retry --alexa-item-id <id>
```

`--show-names` or equivalent explicit opt-in is required before diagnostic commands display full shopping item names. Logs redact item names by default.

### Config

Default config path: `~/.config/alexa-ticktick-bridge/config.toml`.

Default state paths:

```text
~/.local/share/alexa-ticktick-bridge/state.db
~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock
```

Config model must include:

- `poll.interval_seconds`, default `180`, allowed `60..180`
- `poll.jitter_seconds`, default `15`
- `poll.max_backoff_seconds`, default `1800`
- `amazon.domain`, default `co.jp`
- `amazon.list_type`, default `SHOP`
- `amazon.store_password`, default `false`
- `ticktick.client_id`
- `ticktick.redirect_uri`, default `http://127.0.0.1:8765/callback`
- `ticktick.scope`, default `tasks:read tasks:write`
- `ticktick.project_id`
- `slack.enabled`, default `false`
- `slack.redact_item_name`, default `false`
- `storage.sqlite_path`
- `storage.store_plain_item_names`, default `true`
- `logging.level`, default `INFO`
- `logging.redact_item_names`, default `true`

### SecretStore

Define a `SecretStore` interface with `get`, `set`, `delete`, `get_json`, and `set_json`. The default implementation uses OS keyring service `alexa-ticktick-bridge`.

Secret keys:

```text
amazon.email
amazon.login_data
amazon.password
ticktick.client_secret
ticktick.token_response
slack.webhook_url
```

If keyring is unavailable, fail closed by default. Plain secret files are allowed only behind an explicit unsafe flag or command option. Amazon password is stored only when `amazon.store_password=[REDACTED] or an explicit CLI flag requests it.

### TickTick OAuth And Client

Implement authorization-code OAuth for TickTick:

- authorize URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/authorize`
- token URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/token`
- local callback binds only to `127.0.0.1`
- callback validates `state`
- token exchange uses form data and Basic Auth with client id and client secret
- token response is stored in SecretStore as `ticktick.token_response`

Implement a TickTick client for:

- `GET /open/v1/project`
- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}`
- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}/data` for debug or duplicate investigation
- `POST /open/v1/task`

MVP does not need TickTick completion, deletion, or update in the sync path.

### Amazon Auth Adapter

Define adapter interfaces:

- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.domain`
- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.request_json(method, url, payload)`
- `AmazonAuthProvider.login_interactive(email, password, otp)`
- `AmazonAuthProvider.restore()`

MVP implementation may use `aioamazondevices` for login and stored login restore only. Any private-member use required by `aioamazondevices` must remain isolated inside the adapter. The Alexa list client must not import or depend on `aioamazondevices` directly.

Authentication behavior:

- `atb amazon login` prompts for email, password, and current OTP
- successful login stores `amazon.email` and `amazon.login_data`
- default behavior does not store `amazon.password`
- restore uses stored login data
- restore failure raises `ReauthRequired` and daemon exits with a clear reauth message

### Alexa Lists Client

Implement `AlexaListsClient` against the non-official Alexa mobile-app-style list endpoints described in the source spec:

- list fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/fetch`
- items fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/fetch?limit=100`
- complete item: `PUT /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/{itemId}?version={version}`

Models should represent:

- `AlexaListType`: `SHOP`, `TODO`, `CUSTOM`
- `AlexaItemStatus`: `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETE`
- `AlexaList`: `listId`, `listType`, `listName`
- `AlexaItem`: `itemId`, `itemStatus`, `itemName`, `version`

`get_shop_list()` returns the `SHOP` list or raises a typed bridge error. `complete_item()` only updates status to `COMPLETE`; sync must not delete Alexa items.

### Slack Client

Implement Slack Incoming Webhook posting with webhook URL loaded from SecretStore. The webhook URL is never logged or stored in config.

Slack notification failure must not cause duplicate TickTick tasks and must not block Alexa completion after TickTick creation. Rows with missing `slack_notified_at` may be retried later.

### SQLite Store

Create and migrate a SQLite database containing `sync_items` with at least:

- `alexa_item_id` primary key
- `alexa_list_id`
- `alexa_item_name`
- `alexa_item_name_hash`
- `alexa_version`
- `ticktick_project_id`
- `ticktick_task_id`
- `state`
- `ticktick_created_at`
- `slack_notified_at`
- `alexa_completed_at`
- `error_count`
- `last_error`
- `first_seen_at`
- `updated_at`

Required states:

```text
DISCOVERED
TICKTICK_CREATED
SLACK_NOTIFIED
ALEXA_COMPLETED
FAILED_RETRYABLE
FAILED_FATAL
```

The store must support idempotent upsert, mark TickTick created, mark Slack notified, mark Alexa completed, record retryable/fatal errors, and query failed records for status/retry commands.

### SyncService

Required sync order:

1. restore Amazon session
2. load TickTick token
3. get Alexa `SHOP` list
4. get Alexa list items
5. filter `ACTIVE` items
6. upsert discovered SQLite record
7. create TickTick task if `ticktick_task_id` is missing
8. notify Slack if enabled and not already notified
9. mark Alexa item `COMPLETE`
10. mark DB record `ALEXA_COMPLETED`

Idempotency rules:

- `ALEXA_COMPLETED` rows are skipped.
- Existing rows with `ticktick_task_id` must not create another TickTick task.
- If TickTick creation succeeds and Slack fails, rerun retries Slack without duplicating TickTick.
- If TickTick creation succeeds and Alexa complete fails, rerun completes Alexa without duplicating TickTick.
- `--dry-run` must not create TickTick tasks, post Slack messages, complete Alexa items, or mutate external systems.

TickTick task content should include sync metadata:

```text
Synced from Alexa Shopping List

alexa_item_id: <id>
alexa_list_id: <id>
alexa_version_at_sync: <version>
synced_by: alexa_ticktick_bridge
```

### Scheduler And Locking

`atb daemon` uses `SyncService.run_once()` in a loop:

- default interval is 180 seconds
- supported poll interval range is 60 to 180 seconds
- jitter is applied
- 429, 5xx, and network errors use exponential backoff capped by config
- `ReauthRequired` stops the daemon and asks for login
- concurrent `atb daemon` or `atb sync once` runs are blocked by a file lock at `~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock`

On Unix, `fcntl.flock` is sufficient. Windows lock support is not required for MVP.

### Logging And Redaction

Use structured logs and central redaction helpers. Logs must not contain:

- full Amazon email
- Amazon password
- OTP
- Amazon cookie or login data
- TickTick token
- Slack webhook URL
- full item names by default

Provide tests for email masking, item-name masking, and secret redaction.

### Errors

Define typed errors at least:

- `BridgeError`
- `ReauthRequired`
- `AuthFailed`
- `RateLimited`
- `RetryableAPIError`
- `FatalConfigError`
- `VersionConflict`

Expected handling:

- Amazon stored login failure: `ReauthRequired`
- Amazon/TickTick 429: backoff
- network/5xx: retryable
- TickTick 401: `ReauthRequired`
- Slack failure: warning and retry later
- Alexa complete failure: `FAILED_RETRYABLE`
- permanent config/auth issues: `FAILED_FATAL` or explicit CLI error

If scope manager chooses one repo-worker pass, the work item must still require focused checkpoints for each slice and must gate live-network behavior behind explicit user approval.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `pyalexatodo` is not a dependency and no `pyalexatodo` code is copied.
- `aioamazondevices` imports are isolated to Amazon auth adapter implementation.
- `AlexaListsClient` is self-implemented against the endpoints in this plan.
- `atb` CLI exposes init, config, secret, TickTick, Amazon, sync, daemon, status, failed, and retry commands at practical MVP depth.
- Config and state paths match this plan.
- SecretStore defaults to keyring and fails closed if unavailable.
- Amazon password is not persisted by default.
- TickTick task is created before Alexa completion.
- Alexa items are completed, not deleted, after successful TickTick creation.
- Sync is idempotent across reruns and partial failures.
- Slack failure does not duplicate TickTick tasks.
- `--dry-run` performs no external mutation.
- Poll interval is never below 60 seconds.
- Logs redact secrets and item names by default.
- Tests cover OAuth URL/state/token exchange behavior, TickTick client parsing and errors, Alexa client parsing and complete payload, SQLite transitions, SyncService idempotency, dry-run, Slack failure handling, file lock behavior where practical, and redaction.

Repo worker should add automated tests and verifier should run the relevant commands for the final implementation state:


Use mocked HTTP for TickTick, Alexa, and Slack unit tests. Live TickTick tests require explicit environment variables and user approval. Amazon live tests are manual only and must not run in CI.

+- Plan writer does not create work items.
+
+## Public interface and behavior
+
+### Package and CLI
+
+- Package: `alexa_ticktick_bridge`
+- CLI: `atb`
+- Python: `>=3.12`
+- Runtime dependencies from source spec: `aiohttp`, `aioamazondevices`, `pydantic`, `typer`, `rich`, `keyring`, `structlog`
+- Dev dependencies from source spec: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `aioresponses`, `ruff`, `mypy`, `pip-audit`
+- Expected repo layout: `pyproject.toml`, `README.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `.env.example`, `src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/`, and `tests/`.
+
+CLI surface:
+
+```text
+atb init
+atb config show
+atb secret set ticktick.client_secret
+atb secret set slack.webhook_url
+atb secret delete <key>
+atb ticktick login
+atb ticktick projects
+atb ticktick test-create "test item"
+atb amazon login
+atb amazon lists
+atb amazon items
+atb amazon complete --list-id <id> --item-id <id> --version <version>
+atb sync once
+atb sync once --dry-run
+atb daemon
+atb status
+atb failed
+atb retry --alexa-item-id <id>
+```
+
+Diagnostic commands must require `--show-names` or an equivalent explicit option before displaying full shopping item names. Logs redact item names by default.
+
+### Config
+
+Default config path is `~/.config/alexa-ticktick-bridge/config.toml`.
+
+Default state paths:
+
+```text
+~/.local/share/alexa-ticktick-bridge/state.db
+~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock
+```
+
+Config model must include:
+
+- `poll.interval_seconds`, default `180`, allowed `60..180`
+- `poll.jitter_seconds`, default `15`
+- `poll.max_backoff_seconds`, default `1800`
+- `amazon.domain`, default `co.jp`
+- `amazon.list_type`, default `SHOP`
+- `amazon.store_password`, default `false`
+- `ticktick.client_id`
+- `ticktick.redirect_uri`, default `http://127.0.0.1:8765/callback`
+- `ticktick.scope`, default `tasks:read tasks:write`
+- `ticktick.project_id`
+- `slack.enabled`, default `false`
+- `slack.redact_item_name`, default `false`
+- `storage.sqlite_path`
+- `storage.store_plain_item_names`, default `true`
+- `logging.level`, default `INFO`
+- `logging.redact_item_names`, default `true`
+
+### SecretStore
+
+Define a `SecretStore` interface with `get`, `set`, `delete`, `get_json`, and `set_json`. Default implementation uses OS keyring service `alexa-ticktick-bridge`.
+
+Secret keys:
+
+```text
+amazon.email
+amazon.login_data
+amazon.password
+ticktick.client_secret
+ticktick.token_response
+slack.webhook_url
+```
+
+If keyring is unavailable, fail closed by default. Plain secret files are allowed only behind an explicit unsafe flag or command option. Amazon password is stored only when `amazon.store_password=[REDACTED] or an explicit CLI flag requests it.
+
+### TickTick OAuth and client
+
+Implement authorization-code OAuth:
+
+- authorize URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/authorize`
+- token URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/token`
+- local callback binds only to `127.0.0.1`
+- callback validates `state`
+- token exchange uses form data and Basic Auth with client id and client secret
+- token response is stored in SecretStore as `ticktick.token_response`
+
+Implement TickTick API operations:
+
+- `GET /open/v1/project`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}/data` for debug or duplicate investigation
+- `POST /open/v1/task`
+
+MVP does not need TickTick completion, deletion, or update in the sync path.
+
+### Amazon auth adapter
+
+Define adapter interfaces:
+
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.domain`
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.request_json(method, url, payload)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.login_interactive(email, password, otp)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.restore()`
+
+MVP may use `aioamazondevices` for Amazon login and stored login restore only. Any private-member use required by `aioamazondevices` must remain isolated inside the adapter. `AlexaListsClient` must not import or depend on `aioamazondevices` directly.
+
+Authentication behavior:
+
+- `atb amazon login` prompts for email, password, and current OTP
+- successful login stores `amazon.email` and `amazon.login_data`
+- default behavior does not store `amazon.password`
+- restore uses stored login data
+- restore failure raises `ReauthRequired` and daemon exits with a clear reauth message
+
+### Alexa lists client
+
+Implement `AlexaListsClient` against the non-official Alexa mobile-app-style list endpoints described in the source spec:
+
+- list fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/fetch`
+- items fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/fetch?limit=100`
+- complete item: `PUT /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/{itemId}?version={version}`
+
+Models should represent:
+
+- `AlexaListType`: `SHOP`, `TODO`, `CUSTOM`
+- `AlexaItemStatus`: `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETE`
+- `AlexaList`: `listId`, `listType`, `listName`
+- `AlexaItem`: `itemId`, `itemStatus`, `itemName`, `version`
+
+`get_shop_list()` returns the `SHOP` list or raises a typed bridge error. `complete_item()` only updates status to `COMPLETE`; sync must not delete Alexa items.
+
+### Slack client
+
+Implement Slack Incoming Webhook posting with webhook URL loaded from SecretStore. The webhook URL is never logged or stored in config.
+
+Slack notification failure must not cause duplicate TickTick tasks and must not block Alexa completion after TickTick creation. Rows with missing `slack_notified_at` may be retried later.
+
+### SQLite store
+
+Create and migrate a SQLite database containing `sync_items` with at least:
+
+- `alexa_item_id` primary key
+- `alexa_list_id`
+- `alexa_item_name`
+- `alexa_item_name_hash`
+- `alexa_version`
+- `ticktick_project_id`
+- `ticktick_task_id`
+- `state`
+- `ticktick_created_at`
+- `slack_notified_at`
+- `alexa_completed_at`
+- `error_count`
+- `last_error`
+- `first_seen_at`
+- `updated_at`
+
+Required states:
+
+```text
+DISCOVERED
+TICKTICK_CREATED
+SLACK_NOTIFIED
+ALEXA_COMPLETED
+FAILED_RETRYABLE
+FAILED_FATAL
+```
+
+The store must support idempotent upsert, mark TickTick created, mark Slack notified, mark Alexa completed, record retryable/fatal errors, and query failed records for status/retry commands.
+
+### SyncService
+
+Required sync order:
+
+1. restore Amazon session
+2. load TickTick token
+3. get Alexa `SHOP` list
+4. get Alexa list items
+5. filter `ACTIVE` items
+6. upsert discovered SQLite record
+7. create TickTick task if `ticktick_task_id` is missing
+8. notify Slack if enabled and not already notified
+9. mark Alexa item `COMPLETE`
+10. mark DB record `ALEXA_COMPLETED`
+
+Idempotency rules:
+
+- `ALEXA_COMPLETED` rows are skipped.
+- Existing rows with `ticktick_task_id` must not create another TickTick task.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Slack fails, rerun retries Slack without duplicating TickTick.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Alexa complete fails, rerun completes Alexa without duplicating TickTick.
+- `--dry-run` must not create TickTick tasks, post Slack messages, complete Alexa items, or mutate external systems.
+
+TickTick task content should include sync metadata:
+
+```text
+Synced from Alexa Shopping List
+
+alexa_item_id: <id>
+alexa_list_id: <id>
+alexa_version_at_sync: <version>
+synced_by: alexa_ticktick_bridge
+```
+
+### Scheduler, lock, logging, and errors
+
+`atb daemon` runs `SyncService.run_once()` with default 180 second interval, 60 to 180 second interval bounds, jitter, capped exponential backoff for 429/5xx/network errors, and `ReauthRequired` stop behavior.
+
+Concurrent `atb daemon` and `atb sync once` runs are blocked by `~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock`. Unix `fcntl.flock` is sufficient for MVP.
+
+Use structured logs and central redaction. Logs must not contain full Amazon email, Amazon password, OTP, Amazon cookie/login data, TickTick token, Slack webhook URL, or full item names by default.
+
+Define typed errors at least: `BridgeError`, `ReauthRequired`, `AuthFailed`, `RateLimited`, `RetryableAPIError`, `FatalConfigError`, and `VersionConflict`.
+
+## Recommended work-item slices for dlc_scope_manager
+
+The scope manager owns work-items. A feasible one-pass repo worker assignment may combine these if implementation budget allows; otherwise split in this order:
+
+1. Scaffold package and tooling: `pyproject.toml`, source layout, CLI shell, config, errors, logging, SecretStore, README/SECURITY, baseline tests.
+2. TickTick OAuth/client: OAuth URL, callback server, token exchange, project listing, task creation, CLI commands, tests.
+3. Amazon auth adapter and Alexa list client: adapter boundaries, login/restore, list/item fetch, complete item, CLI commands, tests with fixtures.
+4. SQLite store and SyncService: migrations, idempotent transitions, dry-run, retry state, tests.
+5. Slack, scheduler, lock, hardening: webhook client, daemon, backoff/jitter, file lock, redaction coverage, operational docs.
+
+If scope manager chooses one repo-worker pass, the work item must still require focused checkpoints for each slice and gate live-network behavior behind explicit user approval.

+# alexa-ticktick-bridge: Alexa Shopping List to TickTick Bridge
+
+## Goal
+
+- Linear: local://alexa-ticktick-bridge
+- Repositories: alexa_ticktick_bridge
+- Workspace root: /[REDACTED]-ticktick-bridge
+- root-system path: /home/glaucus03/dev/projects/alexatoticktick
+- Base ref: main
+
+## Sprint contract
+
+### Scope
+
+- Implement a private Python 3.12 package named `alexa_ticktick_bridge` and a Typer CLI named `atb` in repo `alexa_ticktick_bridge/`.
+- Use `/[REDACTED].md` as the read-only source specification.
+- Poll Alexa Shopping List items, create TickTick tasks in a configured project, optionally notify Slack, mark Alexa items `COMPLETE` only after successful TickTick creation, and persist idempotent state in SQLite.
+- Do not depend on or copy `pyalexatodo`. Alexa list operations must be self-implemented. Amazon login may use `aioamazondevices` only behind `AmazonAuthProvider` and `AuthenticatedAmazonSession`.
+
+### Exclusions
+
+- No out-of-scope repo edits.
+- No official Alexa List Management REST API dependency.
+- No Alexa item deletion in the sync path.
+- No Amazon password persistence by default.
+- No live Amazon test in CI.
+- No commit, push, root-export, or cleanup without explicit user approval.
+- Plan writer does not create work items.
+
+## Public Interface And Behavior
+
+### Package And CLI
+
+- Package: `alexa_ticktick_bridge`
+- CLI: `atb`
+- Python: `>=3.12`
+- Runtime dependencies from source spec: `aiohttp`, `aioamazondevices`, `pydantic`, `typer`, `rich`, `keyring`, `structlog`
+- Dev dependencies from source spec: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `aioresponses`, `ruff`, `mypy`, `pip-audit`
+- Expected repo layout: `pyproject.toml`, `README.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `.env.example`, `src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/`, and `tests/`.
+
+CLI surface:
+
+```text
+atb init
+atb config show
+atb secret set ticktick.client_secret
+atb secret set slack.webhook_url
+atb secret delete <key>
+atb ticktick login
+atb ticktick projects
+atb ticktick test-create "test item"
+atb amazon login
+atb amazon lists
+atb amazon items
+atb amazon complete --list-id <id> --item-id <id> --version <version>
+atb sync once
+atb sync once --dry-run
+atb daemon
+atb status
+atb failed
+atb retry --alexa-item-id <id>
+```
+
+Diagnostic commands must require `--show-names` or an equivalent explicit option before displaying full shopping item names. Logs redact item names by default.
+
+### Config
+
+Default config path is `~/.config/alexa-ticktick-bridge/config.toml`.
+
+Default state paths:
+
+```text
+~/.local/share/alexa-ticktick-bridge/state.db
+~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock
+```
+
+Config model must include:
+
+- `poll.interval_seconds`, default `180`, allowed `60..180`
+- `poll.jitter_seconds`, default `15`
+- `poll.max_backoff_seconds`, default `1800`
+- `amazon.domain`, default `co.jp`
+- `amazon.list_type`, default `SHOP`
+- `amazon.store_password`, default `false`
+- `ticktick.client_id`
+- `ticktick.redirect_uri`, default `http://127.0.0.1:8765/callback`
+- `ticktick.scope`, default `tasks:read tasks:write`
+- `ticktick.project_id`
+- `slack.enabled`, default `false`
+- `slack.redact_item_name`, default `false`
+- `storage.sqlite_path`
+- `storage.store_plain_item_names`, default `true`
+- `logging.level`, default `INFO`
+- `logging.redact_item_names`, default `true`
+
+### SecretStore
+
+Define a `SecretStore` interface with `get`, `set`, `delete`, `get_json`, and `set_json`. Default implementation uses OS keyring service `alexa-ticktick-bridge`.
+
+Secret keys:
+
+```text
+amazon.email
+amazon.login_data
+amazon.password
+ticktick.client_secret
+ticktick.token_response
+slack.webhook_url
+```
+
+If keyring is unavailable, fail closed by default. Plain secret files are allowed only behind an explicit unsafe flag or command option. Amazon password is stored only when `amazon.store_password=[REDACTED] or an explicit CLI flag requests it.
+
+### TickTick OAuth And Client
+
+Implement authorization-code OAuth:
+
+- authorize URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/authorize`
+- token URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/token`
+- local callback binds only to `127.0.0.1`
+- callback validates `state`
+- token exchange uses form data and Basic Auth with client id and client secret
+- token response is stored in SecretStore as `ticktick.token_response`
+
+Implement TickTick API operations:
+
+- `GET /open/v1/project`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}/data` for debug or duplicate investigation
+- `POST /open/v1/task`
+
+MVP does not need TickTick completion, deletion, or update in the sync path.
+
+### Amazon Auth Adapter
+
+Define adapter interfaces:
+
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.domain`
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.request_json(method, url, payload)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.login_interactive(email, password, otp)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.restore()`
+
+MVP may use `aioamazondevices` for Amazon login and stored login restore only. Any private-member use required by `aioamazondevices` must remain isolated inside the adapter. `AlexaListsClient` must not import or depend on `aioamazondevices` directly.
+
+Authentication behavior:
+
+- `atb amazon login` prompts for email, password, and current OTP
+- successful login stores `amazon.email` and `amazon.login_data`
+- default behavior does not store `amazon.password`
+- restore uses stored login data
+- restore failure raises `ReauthRequired` and daemon exits with a clear reauth message
+
+### Alexa Lists Client
+
+Implement `AlexaListsClient` against the non-official Alexa mobile-app-style list endpoints described in the source spec:
+
+- list fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/fetch`
+- items fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/fetch?limit=100`
+- complete item: `PUT /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/{itemId}?version={version}`
+
+Models should represent:
+
+- `AlexaListType`: `SHOP`, `TODO`, `CUSTOM`
+- `AlexaItemStatus`: `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETE`
+- `AlexaList`: `listId`, `listType`, `listName`
+- `AlexaItem`: `itemId`, `itemStatus`, `itemName`, `version`
+
+`get_shop_list()` returns the `SHOP` list or raises a typed bridge error. `complete_item()` only updates status to `COMPLETE`; sync must not delete Alexa items.
+
+### Slack Client
+
+Implement Slack Incoming Webhook posting with webhook URL loaded from SecretStore. The webhook URL is never logged or stored in config.
+
+Slack notification failure must not cause duplicate TickTick tasks and must not block Alexa completion after TickTick creation. Rows with missing `slack_notified_at` may be retried later.
+
+### SQLite Store
+
+Create and migrate a SQLite database containing `sync_items` with at least:
+
+- `alexa_item_id` primary key
+- `alexa_list_id`
+- `alexa_item_name`
+- `alexa_item_name_hash`
+- `alexa_version`
+- `ticktick_project_id`
+- `ticktick_task_id`
+- `state`
+- `ticktick_created_at`
+- `slack_notified_at`
+- `alexa_completed_at`
+- `error_count`
+- `last_error`
+- `first_seen_at`
+- `updated_at`
+
+Required states:
+
+```text
+DISCOVERED
+TICKTICK_CREATED
+SLACK_NOTIFIED
+ALEXA_COMPLETED
+FAILED_RETRYABLE
+FAILED_FATAL
+```
+
+The store must support idempotent upsert, mark TickTick created, mark Slack notified, mark Alexa completed, record retryable/fatal errors, and query failed records for status/retry commands.
+
+### SyncService
+
+Required sync order:
+
+1. restore Amazon session
+2. load TickTick token
+3. get Alexa `SHOP` list
+4. get Alexa list items
+5. filter `ACTIVE` items
+6. upsert discovered SQLite record
+7. create TickTick task if `ticktick_task_id` is missing
+8. notify Slack if enabled and not already notified
+9. mark Alexa item `COMPLETE`
+10. mark DB record `ALEXA_COMPLETED`
+
+Idempotency rules:
+
+- `ALEXA_COMPLETED` rows are skipped.
+- Existing rows with `ticktick_task_id` must not create another TickTick task.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Slack fails, rerun retries Slack without duplicating TickTick.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Alexa complete fails, rerun completes Alexa without duplicating TickTick.
+- `--dry-run` must not create TickTick tasks, post Slack messages, complete Alexa items, or mutate external systems.
+
+TickTick task content should include sync metadata:
+
+```text
+Synced from Alexa Shopping List
+
+alexa_item_id: <id>
+alexa_list_id: <id>
+alexa_version_at_sync: <version>
+synced_by: alexa_ticktick_bridge
+```
+
+### Scheduler, Locking, Logging, And Errors
+
+`atb daemon` runs `SyncService.run_once()` with default 180 second interval, 60 to 180 second interval bounds, jitter, capped exponential backoff for 429/5xx/network errors, and `ReauthRequired` stop behavior.
+
+Concurrent `atb daemon` and `atb sync once` runs are blocked by `~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock`. Unix `fcntl.flock` is sufficient for MVP.
+
+Use structured logs and central redaction. Logs must not contain full Amazon email, Amazon password, OTP, Amazon cookie/login data, TickTick token, Slack webhook URL, or full item names by default.
+
+Define typed errors at least: `BridgeError`, `ReauthRequired`, `AuthFailed`, `RateLimited`, `RetryableAPIError`, `FatalConfigError`, and `VersionConflict`.
+
+## Recommended Work-Item Slices For dlc_scope_manager
+
+The scope manager owns work-items. A feasible one-pass repo worker assignment may combine these if implementation budget allows; otherwise split in this order:
+
+1. Scaffold package and tooling: `pyproject.toml`, source layout, CLI shell, config, errors, logging, SecretStore, README/SECURITY, baseline tests.
+2. TickTick OAuth/client: OAuth URL, callback server, token exchange, project listing, task creation, CLI commands, tests.
+3. Amazon auth adapter and Alexa list client: adapter boundaries, login/restore, list/item fetch, complete item, CLI commands, tests with fixtures.
+4. SQLite store and SyncService: migrations, idempotent transitions, dry-run, retry state, tests.
+5. Slack, scheduler, lock, hardening: webhook client, daemon, backoff/jitter, file lock, redaction coverage, operational docs.
+
+If scope manager chooses one repo-worker pass, the work item must still require focused checkpoints for each slice and gate live-network behavior behind explicit user approval.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- `pyalexatodo` is not a dependency and no `pyalexatodo` code is copied.
+- `aioamazondevices` imports are isolated to Amazon auth adapter implementation.
+- `AlexaListsClient` is self-implemented against the endpoints in this plan.
+- `atb` CLI exposes init, config, secret, TickTick, Amazon, sync, daemon, status, failed, and retry commands at practical MVP depth.
+- Config and state paths match this plan.
+- SecretStore defaults to keyring and fails closed if unavailable.
+- Amazon password is not persisted by default.
+- TickTick task is created before Alexa completion.
+- Alexa items are completed, not deleted, after successful TickTick creation.

@@
 ### Scope
 
-- Fill active work items before implementation.
+- Implement a private Python 3.12 package named `alexa_ticktick_bridge` and a Typer CLI named `atb` in repo `alexa_ticktick_bridge/`.
+- Use `/[REDACTED].md` as the read-only source specification.
+- Poll Alexa Shopping List items, create TickTick tasks in a configured project, optionally notify Slack, mark Alexa items `COMPLETE` only after successful TickTick creation, and persist idempotent state in SQLite.
+- Do not depend on or copy `pyalexatodo`. Alexa list operations must be self-implemented. Amazon login may use `aioamazondevices` only behind `AmazonAuthProvider` and `AuthenticatedAmazonSession`.
 
 ### Exclusions
 
 - No out-of-scope repo edits.
+- No official Alexa List Management REST API dependency.
+- No Alexa item deletion in the sync path.
+- No Amazon password persistence by default.
+- No live Amazon test in CI.
+- No commit, push, root-export, or cleanup without explicit user approval.
+- Plan writer does not create work items.
+
+## Public Interface And Behavior
+
+### Package And CLI
+
+- Package: `alexa_ticktick_bridge`
+- CLI: `atb`
+- Python: `>=3.12`
+- Runtime dependencies from source spec: `aiohttp`, `aioamazondevices`, `pydantic`, `typer`, `rich`, `keyring`, `structlog`
+- Dev dependencies from source spec: `pytest`, `pytest-asyncio`, `aioresponses`, `ruff`, `mypy`, `pip-audit`
+- Expected repo layout: `pyproject.toml`, `README.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `.env.example`, `src/alexa_ticktick_bridge/`, and `tests/`.
+
+CLI surface:
+
+```text
+atb init
+atb config show
+atb secret set ticktick.client_secret
+atb secret set slack.webhook_url
+atb secret delete <key>
+atb ticktick login
+atb ticktick projects
+atb ticktick test-create "test item"
+atb amazon login
+atb amazon lists
+atb amazon items
+atb amazon complete --list-id <id> --item-id <id> --version <version>
+atb sync once
+atb sync once --dry-run
+atb daemon
+atb status
+atb failed
+atb retry --alexa-item-id <id>
+```
+
+Diagnostic commands must require `--show-names` or an equivalent explicit option before displaying full shopping item names. Logs redact item names by default.
+
+### Config
+
+Default config path is `~/.config/alexa-ticktick-bridge/config.toml`.
+
+Default state paths:
+
+```text
+~/.local/share/alexa-ticktick-bridge/state.db
+~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock
+```
+
+Config model must include:
+
+- `poll.interval_seconds`, default `180`, allowed `60..180`
+- `poll.jitter_seconds`, default `15`
+- `poll.max_backoff_seconds`, default `1800`
+- `amazon.domain`, default `co.jp`
+- `amazon.list_type`, default `SHOP`
+- `amazon.store_password`, default `false`
+- `ticktick.client_id`
+- `ticktick.redirect_uri`, default `http://127.0.0.1:8765/callback`
+- `ticktick.scope`, default `tasks:read tasks:write`
+- `ticktick.project_id`
+- `slack.enabled`, default `false`
+- `slack.redact_item_name`, default `false`
+- `storage.sqlite_path`
+- `storage.store_plain_item_names`, default `true`
+- `logging.level`, default `INFO`
+- `logging.redact_item_names`, default `true`
+
+### SecretStore
+
+Define a `SecretStore` interface with `get`, `set`, `delete`, `get_json`, and `set_json`. Default implementation uses OS keyring service `alexa-ticktick-bridge`.
+
+Secret keys:
+
+```text
+amazon.email
+amazon.login_data
+amazon.password
+ticktick.client_secret
+ticktick.token_response
+slack.webhook_url
+```
+
+If keyring is unavailable, fail closed by default. Plain secret files are allowed only behind an explicit unsafe flag or command option. Amazon password is stored only when `amazon.store_password=[REDACTED] or an explicit CLI flag requests it.
+
+### TickTick OAuth And Client
+
+Implement authorization-code OAuth:
+
+- authorize URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/authorize`
+- token URL: `https://ticktick.com/oauth/token`
+- local callback binds only to `127.0.0.1`
+- callback validates `state`
+- token exchange uses form data and Basic Auth with client id and client secret
+- token response is stored in SecretStore as `ticktick.token_response`
+
+Implement TickTick API operations:
+
+- `GET /open/v1/project`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}`
+- `GET /open/v1/project/{projectId}/data` for debug or duplicate investigation
+- `POST /open/v1/task`
+
+MVP does not need TickTick completion, deletion, or update in the sync path.
+
+### Amazon Auth Adapter
+
+Define adapter interfaces:
+
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.domain`
+- `AuthenticatedAmazonSession.request_json(method, url, payload)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.login_interactive(email, password, otp)`
+- `AmazonAuthProvider.restore()`
+
+MVP may use `aioamazondevices` for Amazon login and stored login restore only. Any private-member use required by `aioamazondevices` must remain isolated inside the adapter. `AlexaListsClient` must not import or depend on `aioamazondevices` directly.
+
+Authentication behavior:
+
+- `atb amazon login` prompts for email, password, and current OTP
+- successful login stores `amazon.email` and `amazon.login_data`
+- default behavior does not store `amazon.password`
+- restore uses stored login data
+- restore failure raises `ReauthRequired` and daemon exits with a clear reauth message
+
+### Alexa Lists Client
+
+Implement `AlexaListsClient` against the non-official Alexa mobile-app-style list endpoints described in the source spec:
+
+- list fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/fetch`
+- items fetch: `POST /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/fetch?limit=100`
+- complete item: `PUT /alexashoppinglists/api/v2/lists/{listId}/items/{itemId}?version={version}`
+
+Models should represent:
+
+- `AlexaListType`: `SHOP`, `TODO`, `CUSTOM`
+- `AlexaItemStatus`: `ACTIVE`, `COMPLETE`
+- `AlexaList`: `listId`, `listType`, `listName`
+- `AlexaItem`: `itemId`, `itemStatus`, `itemName`, `version`
+
+`get_shop_list()` returns the `SHOP` list or raises a typed bridge error. `complete_item()` only updates status to `COMPLETE`; sync must not delete Alexa items.
+
+### Slack Client
+
+Implement Slack Incoming Webhook posting with webhook URL loaded from SecretStore. The webhook URL is never logged or stored in config.
+
+Slack notification failure must not cause duplicate TickTick tasks and must not block Alexa completion after TickTick creation. Rows with missing `slack_notified_at` may be retried later.
+
+### SQLite Store
+
+Create and migrate a SQLite database containing `sync_items` with at least:
+
+- `alexa_item_id` primary key
+- `alexa_list_id`
+- `alexa_item_name`
+- `alexa_item_name_hash`
+- `alexa_version`
+- `ticktick_project_id`
+- `ticktick_task_id`
+- `state`
+- `ticktick_created_at`
+- `slack_notified_at`
+- `alexa_completed_at`
+- `error_count`
+- `last_error`
+- `first_seen_at`
+- `updated_at`
+
+Required states:
+
+```text
+DISCOVERED
+TICKTICK_CREATED
+SLACK_NOTIFIED
+ALEXA_COMPLETED
+FAILED_RETRYABLE
+FAILED_FATAL
+```
+
+The store must support idempotent upsert, mark TickTick created, mark Slack notified, mark Alexa completed, record retryable/fatal errors, and query failed records for status/retry commands.
+
+### SyncService
+
+Required sync order:
+
+1. restore Amazon session
+2. load TickTick token
+3. get Alexa `SHOP` list
+4. get Alexa list items
+5. filter `ACTIVE` items
+6. upsert discovered SQLite record
+7. create TickTick task if `ticktick_task_id` is missing
+8. notify Slack if enabled and not already notified
+9. mark Alexa item `COMPLETE`
+10. mark DB record `ALEXA_COMPLETED`
+
+Idempotency rules:
+
+- `ALEXA_COMPLETED` rows are skipped.
+- Existing rows with `ticktick_task_id` must not create another TickTick task.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Slack fails, rerun retries Slack without duplicating TickTick.
+- If TickTick creation succeeds and Alexa complete fails, rerun completes Alexa without duplicating TickTick.
+- `--dry-run` must not create TickTick tasks, post Slack messages, complete Alexa items, or mutate external systems.
+
+TickTick task content should include sync metadata:
+
+```text
+Synced from Alexa Shopping List
+
+alexa_item_id: <id>
+alexa_list_id: <id>
+alexa_version_at_sync: <version>
+synced_by: alexa_ticktick_bridge
+```
+
+### Scheduler, Locking, Logging, And Errors
+
+`atb daemon` runs `SyncService.run_once()` with default 180 second interval, 60 to 180 second interval bounds, jitter, capped exponential backoff for 429/5xx/network errors, and `ReauthRequired` stop behavior.
+
+Concurrent `atb daemon` and `atb sync once` runs are blocked by `~/.local/state/alexa-ticktick-bridge/run.lock`. Unix `fcntl.flock` is sufficient for MVP.
+
+Use structured logs and central redaction. Logs must not contain full Amazon email, Amazon password, OTP, Amazon cookie/login data, TickTick token, Slack webhook URL, or full item names by default.
+
+Define typed errors at least: `BridgeError`, `ReauthRequired`, `AuthFailed`, `RateLimited`, `RetryableAPIError`, `FatalConfigError`, and `VersionConflict`.
+
+## Recommended Work-Item Slices For dlc_scope_manager
+
+The scope manager owns work-items. A feasible one-pass repo worker assignment may combine these if implementation budget allows; otherwise split in this order:
+
+1. Scaffold package and tooling: `pyproject.toml`, source layout, CLI shell, config, errors, logging, SecretStore, README/SECURITY, baseline tests.
+2. TickTick OAuth/client: OAuth URL, callback server, token exchange, project listing, task creation, CLI commands, tests.
+3. Amazon auth adapter and Alexa list client: adapter boundaries, login/restore, list/item fetch, complete item, CLI commands, tests with fixtures.
+4. SQLite store and SyncService: migrations, idempotent transitions, dry-run, retry state, tests.
+5. Slack, scheduler, lock, hardening: webhook client, daemon, backoff/jitter, file lock, redaction coverage, operational docs.
+
+If scope manager chooses one repo-worker pass, the work item must still require focused checkpoints for each slice and gate live-network behavior behind explicit user approval.
*** End Patch

plan_ref: ai-dlc/plans/alexa-ticktick-bridge.md
wip_limit: 1
active_item: WI-001
items:
- id: WI-001
  title: Implement Alexa Shopping List to TickTick Bridge MVP
  state: active
  status: active
  assignment_role: dlc_repo_worker
  repo: alexa_ticktick_bridge
  repo_path: alexa_ticktick_bridge/
  source_ref: ai-dlc/plans/alexa-ticktick-bridge.md
  implementation_source_ref: /[REDACTED].md
  summary: Build the private Python 3.12 package and Typer CLI described by the approved plan, with mocked automated coverage and no live service calls unless separately approved.
  writable:
  - alexa_ticktick_bridge/**
  forbidden:
  - ai-dlc/**
  - workspace.yaml
  - .codex/**
  - ../.local/**
  - .git/**
  - PLAN.md
  - worktrees/**
  checkpoints:
  - Scaffold pyproject, README, SECURITY, .env.example, src layout, tests layout, config model, typed errors, logging redaction, SecretStore, and CLI shell.
  - Implement TickTick OAuth, token storage, project listing, task creation, CLI commands, and mocked tests.
  - Implement Amazon auth adapter boundary, self-owned Alexa list client endpoints, list/item/complete CLI commands, and mocked fixtures. Do not import aioamazondevices outside the auth adapter.
  - Implement SQLite migrations, idempotent state transitions, SyncService ordering, dry-run behavior, failed/status/retry commands, and idempotency tests.
  - Implement Slack webhook behavior, daemon scheduling, file lock, backoff/jitter, hardening docs, and redaction/security tests.
  acceptance_criteria:
  - pyalexatodo is not a dependency and no pyalexatodo code is copied.
  - aioamazondevices imports are isolated to the Amazon auth adapter; AlexaListsClient is self-implemented.
  - atb exposes init, config, secret, ticktick, amazon, sync, daemon, status, failed, and retry commands at MVP depth.
  - SecretStore defaults to keyring and fails closed if keyring is unavailable.
  - Amazon password is not persisted by default.
  - TickTick task creation happens before Alexa completion, and Alexa items are completed rather than deleted.
  - Sync remains idempotent across reruns and partial failures, including Slack or Alexa completion failures.
  - Dry-run performs no external mutation.
  - Poll interval is bounded at a minimum of 60 seconds.
  - Logs redact secrets and item names by default.
  blocked_by: []
  approval_gates:
  - Dependency installation or dependency download is approval-gated.
  - Live TickTick validation is approval-gated and requires explicit credentials/environment.
  - Live Amazon tests are manual-only and approval-gated; do not run them in CI.
  - Commit, push, PR creation, root-export, overlay cleanup, and destructive cleanup are forbidden until explicit ready_to_finish approval.
  repo_worker_assignment_boundary:
    role: dlc_repo_worker
    repo: alexa_ticktick_bridge
    repo_path: alexa_ticktick_bridge/
    work_item: WI-001
    writable:
    - alexa_ticktick_bridge/**
    forbidden:
    - ai-dlc/**
    - workspace.yaml
    - .codex/**
    - ../.local/**
    - .git/**
    - PLAN.md
    - worktrees/**
  deliverables:
  - worker_report
  - verifier_evidence
  - evaluator_verdict
  verifier_gate:
    phase: verifying
    assignment_role: dlc_verifier
    required: true
    required_commands:
    - cwd: alexa_ticktick_bridge/
      command: ruff check .
    - cwd: alexa_ticktick_bridge/
      command: mypy .
    - cwd: alexa_ticktick_bridge/
      command: pytest
    recommended_commands:
    - cwd: alexa_ticktick_bridge/
      command: pip-audit
    evidence_required:
    - command logs under ../.local/ai-dlc/logs/
    - verified branch and HEAD SHA
    - tree cleanliness state
    notes:
    - Use mocked HTTP for TickTick, Alexa, and Slack automated tests.
    - If dependencies are missing, stop for approval before installing or downloading them.
  evaluator_gate:
    phase: evaluating
    assignment_role: dlc_evaluator
    required: true
    checks:
    - Verify acceptance criteria against the approved plan and worker report.
    - Confirm verifier evidence exists before evaluator verdict.
    - Confirm no out-of-scope workspace or control-plane paths were edited by the repo worker.
candidate_strategy:
  selected: one comprehensive MVP repo-worker item
  rationale: The repo is greenfield and the plan allows one worker pass if slice checkpoints and verifier gates remain explicit.
  active:
  - WI-001
  deferred: []
  included_slices:
  - S1-scaffold-package-tooling
  - S2-ticktick-oauth-client
  - S3-amazon-auth-alexa-client
  - S4-sqlite-store-sync-service
  - S5-slack-scheduler-lock-hardening
next_recommended_status:
  plan_status: executing
  owner: dlc_repo_worker
  action: implement WI-001 within alexa_ticktick_bridge/ only
'''
Path('ai-dlc/work-items/alexa-ticktick-bridge.yaml').write_text(content, encoding='utf-8')"
